message:
chore: sync beads issue tracker and update README for OpenCode provider

- Update beads JSONL with current issue state
- Add OpenCode to the supported providers list in README

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -14,12 +14,12 @@
 {"id":"bd-20x.1","title":"Implement Claude Code session reader","description":"Implement Provider::read_session() for Claude Code in src/providers/claude_code.rs by porting/adapting CASS claude_code connector behavior.

Format scope:
- Scan Claude session files under ~/.claude/projects/* (JSONL primary; JSON/.claude variants where present).
- Parse user/assistant conversational events while skipping non-conversational event types.
- Resolve role/content/timestamp using robust fallback rules from CASS helpers.
- Extract workspace/session metadata (cwd, sessionId, gitBranch) and preserve raw event data in extra.
- Derive external session identity from filename/path conventions used by Claude files.

Robustness requirements:
- Tolerate malformed lines/files without crashing; keep valid messages.
- Skip empty/whitespace content messages.
- Preserve ordering and stable message indexing after filtering.
- Handle large files safely and log skip decisions.

Parity requirements:
- Behavior should align with CASS porting notes unless an intentional divergence is documented.
- Any divergence must be captured in docs/cass-porting-notes.md with rationale.

Acceptance criteria:
- Reader passes fixture tests (synthetic + redacted-real Claude fixtures).
- Reader passes CASS parity regression tests for Claude fixtures.
- Metadata/workspace/title extraction behavior is covered by unit tests with detailed failure logs.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:40:30.002441671Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:53:53.314938750Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:53:53.314919744Z","close_reason":"Implemented Claude Code JSONL reader with: conversational message filtering (user/assistant only), metadata extraction (sessionId, cwd, gitBranch, version), content flattening, tool call/result extraction, timestamp tracking, model name counting. Handles malformed lines gracefully. Clippy+fmt clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["claude-code","readers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20x.1","depends_on_id":"bd-20x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:09.632577598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20x.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:33.903049475Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20x.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:33.982596617Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-20x.2","title":"Implement Codex session reader","description":"Implement Provider::read_session() for Codex in src/providers/codex.rs by porting/adapting CASS codex connector behavior.

Format scope:
- Scan rollout files under ~/.codex/sessions (JSONL modern + legacy JSON format support).
- JSONL parsing:
  - session_meta: extract workspace/cwd context
  - response_item: parse primary user/assistant turns
  - event_msg user_message/agent_reasoning/token_count handling with CASS-compatible rules
- Legacy JSON parsing: {session, items} fallback handling.

Critical behavior to preserve:
- Token_count events attach retroactively to latest non-reasoning assistant turn.
- Content flattening covers string and block-array representations.
- External ID derivation preserves relative rollout path identity.
- Unknown/non-conversational events are ignored safely.

Robustness requirements:
- Malformed lines are tolerated; valid messages still parsed.
- Empty/whitespace content turns are skipped.
- Message ordering/indexing remains stable after filtering.

Parity requirements:
- Match CASS codex connector behavior unless intentional divergence is documented.
- Record divergences in docs/cass-porting-notes.md.

Acceptance criteria:
- Reader passes synthetic + redacted-real Codex fixture tests.
- Reader passes CASS parity regression tests for Codex fixtures.
- Token usage attachment and legacy-format handling are explicitly unit-tested with detailed logs.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:40:41.242903383Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:58:11.187557755Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:58:11.187534391Z","close_reason":"Implemented Codex JSONL+legacy JSON reader. Modern JSONL: session_meta, response_item, event_msg (user_message/agent_reasoning) parsing. Legacy JSON: session.cwd + items array. Tool call/result extraction, timestamp tracking, graceful malformed-line handling. Clippy+fmt clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["codex","readers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20x.2","depends_on_id":"bd-20x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:09.428446201Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20x.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:34.059624423Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20x.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:34.135701911Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-20x.3","title":"Implement Gemini CLI session reader","description":"Implement Provider::read_session() for Gemini in src/providers/gemini.rs by porting/adapting CASS gemini connector behavior.

Format scope:
- Scan session-*.json files in ~/.gemini/tmp/*/chats/ only.
- Parse top-level session metadata (sessionId, projectHash, start/lastUpdated).
- Parse messages array with Gemini type semantics (user/model -> user/assistant canonical roles).
- Preserve grounding/citation metadata in message extras.

Workspace inference requirements:
- Use CASS-derived extraction cascade from message content (AGENTS header, working-directory pattern, /data/projects heuristic).
- Normalize Unix/Windows path forms robustly.
- Fall back to hash-directory parent when content extraction fails.

Robustness requirements:
- Tolerate malformed files/records without crashing.
- Skip empty/whitespace message content.
- Maintain stable message order and indexing.

Parity requirements:
- Match CASS gemini connector behavior unless intentional divergence is documented.
- Record divergences in docs/cass-porting-notes.md.

Acceptance criteria:
- Reader passes synthetic + redacted-real Gemini fixture tests.
- Reader passes CASS parity regression tests for Gemini fixtures.
- Workspace inference edge cases (quoted paths, Windows paths, truncated project paths) are covered by unit tests with detailed logs.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:40:52.277115849Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:58:14.654942020Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:58:14.654905222Z","close_reason":"Implemented Gemini JSON reader. Parses session-level fields (sessionId, startTime, lastUpdated, projectHash), messages array with type=user/model, content flattening, workspace extraction from message content heuristics. Clippy+fmt clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gemini","readers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20x.3","depends_on_id":"bd-20x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:09.227223379Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20x.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:34.214528815Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20x.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:34.294529466Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-20x.4","title":"Add support for additional providers (Cline, Amp, Aider, Cursor)","description":"After the initial three providers (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini) are complete and stable, extend casr to additional providers where formats are known or can be reverse-engineered.

Candidate providers (priority order):
1. Cursor (VS Code globalStorage)
2. Cline (VS Code globalStorage)
3. Aider (Markdown history format)
4. Amp (VS Code + local share paths)
5. OpenCode (SQLite-backed sessions)

Architecture and dependency policy:
- Implement each as a native casr provider module via the same `Provider` trait.
- Port/adapt CASS insights by vendoring logic as needed.
- Do NOT add runtime dependency on CASS.

Quality bar per provider:
- Reader implementation with fixture coverage.
- Writer implementation that produces provider-plausible artifacts.
- `owns_session` discovery support.
- Resume command wiring and CLI integration.
- Round-trip + e2e coverage with detailed logs.
- Document known lossy conversions and unsupported fields.

Execution strategy:
- Ship providers incrementally behind clear task boundaries and tests.
- Do not merge a provider without passing provider-specific reader/writer + e2e smoke coverage.

## Success Criteria

- Each added provider has:
  - detection + ownership lookup
  - reader + writer
  - resume command wiring
  - fixtures + unit tests + round-trip tests + e2e coverage
- Known lossy fields are explicitly documented and asserted in tests.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:40.707719677Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:56:21.796394043Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["future","readers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4","depends_on_id":"bd-20x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:15.308089591Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4","depends_on_id":"bd-20x.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:57.923127573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4","depends_on_id":"bd-20x.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:58.003187886Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4","depends_on_id":"bd-20x.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:58.078153512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

4. Amp (VS Code + local share paths)
5. OpenCode (SQLite-backed sessions)

- Port/adapt CASS insights by vendoring logic as needed.
- Do NOT add runtime dependency on CASS.

Quality bar per provider:

- Resume command wiring and CLI integration.
- Round-trip + e2e coverage with detailed logs.
- Document known lossy conversions and unsupported fields.

Execution strategy:

- Do not merge a provider without passing provider-specific reader/writer + e2e smoke coverage.

## Success Criteria

- Each added provider has:
  - detection + ownership lookup
  - reader + writer
  - resume command wiring
  - fixtures + unit tests + round-trip tests + e2e coverage
- Known lossy fields are explicitly documented and asserted in tests.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:40.707719677Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T18:47:12.242766130Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["future","readers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4","depends_on_id":"bd-20x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:15.308089591Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4","depends_on_id":"bd-20x.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:57.923127573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4","depends_on_id":"bd-20x.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:58.003187886Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4","depends_on_id":"bd-20x.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:58.078153512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-20x.4.1","title":"Implement Cursor provider adapter (reader, writer, discovery, resume wiring)","description":"Implement a full Cursor provider adapter in casr via the `Provider` trait.

Scope:
- Detection: locate Cursor storage roots (VS Code `globalStorage` patterns) with env override support.
- Discovery: `owns_session(session_id)` fast-path lookup + fallback where needed.
- Reader: parse native Cursor session format into `CanonicalSession` with maximal fidelity.
- Writer: export `CanonicalSession` back into a Cursor-plausible session artifact.
- Resume command generation + CLI integration.
- Provider-specific fixtures + unit tests with detailed logs on parse/write failures.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Cursor provider passes:
  - reader fixture tests,
  - writer read-back tests,
  - round-trip matrix paths involving Cursor (as added),
  - e2e coverage in `scripts/e2e_test.sh` for Cursor paths.
- Provider preserves Cursor-specific metadata where possible and documents lossy fields.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:01:39.641690798Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T16:53:47.142406070Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T16:53:47.142384760Z","close_reason":"Implemented Cursor provider integration, tests, and docs updates","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cursor","future","readers","writers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-20x.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:01:39.641690798Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:56:31.729456723Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-20x.4.2","title":"Implement Cline provider adapter (reader, writer, discovery, resume wiring)","description":"Implement a full Cline provider adapter in casr via the `Provider` trait.

Scope:
- Detection: locate Cline storage roots (VS Code `globalStorage` patterns) with env override support.
- Discovery: `owns_session(session_id)` fast-path lookup + fallback.
- Reader: parse native Cline sessions into `CanonicalSession`.
- Writer: export canonical sessions into Cline-plausible artifacts.
- Resume command support + CLI wiring.
- Provider-specific fixtures + tests with detailed logging.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Cline provider passes reader fixture tests, writer read-back tests, and round-trip paths.
- Detection and ownership are deterministic and debuggable from logs.
- Lossy fields are documented and asserted in tests.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:01:39.723249861Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T16:54:14.017399783Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cline","future","readers","writers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-20x.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:01:39.723249861Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:56:34.583467419Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-20x.4.3","title":"Implement Aider provider adapter (Markdown history import/export + discovery)","description":"Implement Aider provider support.

Scope:
- Discovery: locate Aider history files (Markdown transcripts) and determine a stable session id scheme.
- Reader: robust Markdown transcript parsing into canonical IR (including edge-case markdown, code fences, interleaved tool output sections).
- Writer: export canonical sessions back into a provider-compatible transcript (as close to Aider-native as feasible).
- Tests: fixtures for multiple transcript variants + e2e compatibility tests.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Reader handles real-world markdown edge cases without panicking and produces deterministic canonical output.
- Writer produces Aider-compatible transcript artifacts and passes read-back compatibility tests.
- Fixtures + tests document any lossy conversions explicitly.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:01:39.802863140Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T17:45:40.180022159Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T17:45:40.180004536Z","close_reason":"Implemented Aider provider: detection, discovery, Markdown reader/writer, resume command, CLI wiring (aid alias), 27 unit tests. All cargo check/clippy/test pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aider","future","readers","writers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-20x.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:01:39.802863140Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:56:37.260788020Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-20x.4.4","title":"Implement Amp provider adapter (VS Code + local-share session formats)","description":"Implement Amp provider support across known storage locations.

Scope:
- Discovery probes across known Amp storage layouts (VS Code + local-share paths where applicable).
- Reader/writer behavior via the `Provider` trait.
- Tests covering multi-location resolution, conflicts, and deterministic ownership lookup.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Detection covers all documented Amp storage roots with clear evidence.
- Reader and writer pass fixture + read-back tests.
- Discovery ambiguity is handled deterministically (error + remediation).","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:01:39.882576356Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T17:46:10.572762082Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["amp","future","readers","writers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-20x.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:01:39.882576356Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:56:40.666204767Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-20x.4.5","title":"Implement OpenCode provider adapter (SQLite-backed session support)","description":"Implement OpenCode provider support for SQLite-backed sessions.

Scope:
- Detection: locate OpenCode SQLite DB(s) and identify schema/version.
- Reader: safe read paths into canonical IR (no destructive queries).
- Writer: write semantics for creating new sessions (migration-safe, versioned where needed).
- Discovery: session lookup by id with deterministic mapping.
- Tests: fixtures with minimal SQLite DB snapshots + migration/version fallback behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Reader can parse sessions from a fixture DB and preserves provider-specific metadata.
- Writer produces new sessions without corrupting existing DB state.
- Tests cover schema/version changes with clear diagnostics.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:01:39.962903102Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T18:32:38.177680943Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["future","opencode","readers","writers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-20x.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:01:39.962903102Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:56:44.238849451Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

## Acceptance Criteria

- Writer produces new sessions without corrupting existing DB state.
- Tests cover schema/version changes with clear diagnostics.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:01:39.962903102Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T18:46:02.660001502Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T18:46:02.659980072Z","close_reason":"Completed: OpenCode provider adapter implemented with tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["future","opencode","readers","writers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-20x.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:01:39.962903102Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:56:44.238849451Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-20x.4.6","title":"Add shared fixture and fidelity test matrix for additional providers","description":"Create a shared fixture corpus and fidelity matrix tests for all newly added providers.

Scope:
- Add provider-native fixtures for each new provider (synthetic + redacted-real where possible).
- Extend round-trip and conformance tests to include the new providers.
- Ensure e2e coverage with detailed logs and artifact dumps on failure.
- Document unsupported or lossy conversions per provider with explicit assertions.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Each new provider ships with a fixture set + reader tests + writer tests.
- Round-trip matrix includes new provider paths with explicit lossiness documentation.
- e2e script covers at least one conversion path per new provider.
- Failures publish artifacts (generated output, diffs, logs) sufficient for debugging.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:01:40.042886594Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:57:26.489733575Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["future","providers","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4.6","depends_on_id":"bd-20x.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:01:40.042886594Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4.6","depends_on_id":"bd-20x.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:01:40.042886594Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4.6","depends_on_id":"bd-20x.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:01:40.042886594Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4.6","depends_on_id":"bd-20x.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:01:40.042886594Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4.6","depends_on_id":"bd-20x.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:01:40.042886594Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4.6","depends_on_id":"bd-20x.4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:01:40.042886594Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-24z","title":"EPIC: Testing & Validation","description":"Comprehensive testing and validation for casr.

Required coverage dimensions:
1. Unit tests for each reader and writer.
2. Round-trip invariants (`read->canonical->write->read`) including per-provider and cross-provider paths.
3. Integration tests using realistic fixture corpora for all supported providers.
4. Property-based tests for normalization and parse/serialize resilience.
5. End-to-end CLI tests and shell e2e scripts with detailed structured logging.
6. Real-provider smoke checks (opt-in) that validate resume acceptance with installed provider CLIs.
7. JSON contract tests for `--json` output schemas and error envelopes.
8. Failure-injection tests for atomic-write + rollback semantics.
9. CASS parity regression tests for reader behavior on canonical fixture sets.

Non-negotiable logging/debuggability requirements:
- Every failing test must surface enough context to debug without reproducing manually.
- e2e scripts must output command, expected/actual, artifact paths, and pass/fail summary.
- `VERBOSE`/`TRACE` modes must be test-covered and produce inspectable diagnostics.

Key invariant:
For each provider `P`, `read_P(write_P(canonical))` should be equivalent to canonical (modulo documented provider-specific lossy fields).

## Success Criteria

- CI runs unit, round-trip, contracts, conformance, parity, e2e, and perf suites with actionable artifacts.
- A failing test provides enough logs/artifacts to root-cause without re-running locally.
- Writer correctness is validated by both reader round-trip and golden-output conformance (symmetric-bug protection).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:38:22.669687933Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T16:31:45.703401668Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T16:31:45.703321047Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24z","depends_on_id":"bd-1kg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:05.290967374Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24z","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:05.213287848Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-24z.1","title":"Create test fixtures for each provider format","description":"Create a high-fidelity fixture corpus under tests/fixtures/ for provider readers, writers, round-trip tests, and e2e harnesses.

Fixture strategy (MANDATORY):
1. Synthetic fixtures: minimal and edge-case sessions crafted by hand.
2. Redacted-real fixtures: sanitized snapshots derived from real provider session files so writer/reader behavior is anchored to real-world structures.

Provider-native fixture sets:
- Claude Code: simple, complex (content arrays/tool events), missing-workspace, unicode.
- Codex: modern JSONL, legacy JSON, token_count events, reasoning events.
- Gemini: simple, grounding/citations, missing-workspace inference.
- Cross-cutting edge fixtures: empty/whitespace content, null/missing timestamps, long message bodies, single-sided conversations.

Corpus rules:
- Keep provider fixture files strictly provider-native JSON/JSONL (no inline comment lines).
- Put fixture intent and caveats in sidecar notes files and a manifest (e.g., fixtures_manifest.json).
- For each fixture, include expected canonical summary output used by tests (message count/order/roles, workspace, metadata expectations, known lossy fields).
- Include at least one fixture per provider that exercises malformed-line tolerance without breaking parse of valid remaining messages.

Logging/debuggability requirements:
- Tests consuming fixtures must print fixture ID/path when failing.
- Mismatch reports must include expected vs actual canonical summary artifact paths.

Acceptance criteria:
- Fixture corpus includes both synthetic and redacted-real sets for Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini.
- Every fixture has a sidecar expected canonical summary and documented intent.
- No provider-native fixture relies on non-native comment syntax.
- Fixture IDs are stable and referenced by reader/writer/round-trip/e2e tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:42:56.641976155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T13:26:41.512839113Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T13:26:41.512621646Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24z.1","depends_on_id":"bd-24z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:13.061662605Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24z.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:07.867670879Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
